Bookmarks that match an autofill rule bu: Bookmarks that match an autofill rule but haven't had its prefill values applied yet. You can apply rules in bulk or exempt individual bookmarks.
Bookmarks whose category was removed and: Bookmarks whose category was removed and never reassigned.

Browse the Genres & Moods taxonomy — a s: Browse the Genres & Moods taxonomy — a single hierarchical vocabulary you can apply to bookmarks and any other taxonomy. Click an entry to view or edit it.
Browse the Groups taxonomy. Create a gro: Browse the Groups taxonomy. Create a group, then assign them to bookmarks.
Browse the Imports taxonomy. Create an i: Browse the Imports taxonomy. Create an import, set its default category, tags, and media type, then select it when adding an import. Click an import to browse its import groups.
Browse the Locations taxonomy. Use “New: Browse the Locations taxonomy. Use “New location” to add a place — look it up to autograb its coordinates, and add higher-level locations in the same step. Click a location to view or edit it.
Browse the Media Types taxonomy. Built-i: Browse the Media Types taxonomy. Built-in types ship with the app; add your own below. Click a type to view or edit it.
Browse the People taxonomy. Create an pe: Browse the People taxonomy. Create an person, then assign them to bookmarks.
Browse the Websites taxonomy. Sites are: Browse the Websites taxonomy. Sites are created automatically when you add bookmarks. Click a site to view or edit it.
Browse the YouTube Channels taxonomy. Ch: Browse the YouTube Channels taxonomy. Channels are created automatically when you add YouTube bookmarks. Click a channel to view or edit it.
Browse the tag taxonomy. Click a tag to: Browse the tag taxonomy. Click a tag to view and edit it.
Browse which languages are used at each: Browse which languages are used at each level. Edit the vocabulary itself on the Edit page.

Category applied automatically to bookma: Category applied automatically to bookmarks saved from this channel.

Choose a bookmark to attach this orphane: Choose a bookmark to attach this orphaned image to. This will set (or replace) that bookmark's image.

Choose a section to manage your preferen: Choose a section to manage your preferences.
Choose an icon and a color for each plac: Choose an icon and a color for each place type. The icon is drawn inside the pin on the Locations map; the color overrides the level-group color for that place type.
Choose an image to upload, or fetch one: Choose an image to upload, or fetch one from the source. Stored as a WebP.
Choose how each category appears in the: Choose how each category appears in the left sidebar.
Choose how each connector link appears i: Choose how each connector link appears in the left sidebar's Connectors section.
Choose how each customization tool appea: Choose how each customization tool appears in the left sidebar.
Choose how each taxonomy browser appears: Choose how each taxonomy browser appears in the left sidebar.
Choose how this section's bookmarks are: Choose how this section's bookmarks are ordered. Uses the same sort options as the Listings page.
Choose tags, then add or remove them acr: Choose tags, then add or remove them across {{count}} selected {{noun}}.
Choose the language the interface is dis: Choose the language the interface is displayed in.
Choose where each Add Bookmark form fiel: Choose where each Add Bookmark form field appears — in the main area (Default), tucked into the collapsible Advanced section, or hidden. The URL field is always shown. Placement only affects the Add Bookmark form; every field stays editable after a bookmark is created.
Choose which bookmarks appear in this se: Choose which bookmarks appear in this section. Combine conditions with AND/OR.
Choose which categories offer this tag w: Choose which categories offer this tag when tagging a bookmark. Checking a category that currently allows every tag will limit it to an explicit list. Use “All categories” to offer this tag in every category at once.
Choose which categories this property ap: Choose which categories this property applies to.
Choose which fields the quick Add Bookma: Choose which fields the quick Add Bookmark form shows, and where.
Choose which filters show by default in: Choose which filters show by default in the filter sidebar. Filters set to “On demand” are hidden until you add them from the “Add filter” control.
Choose which groups and items appear in: Choose which groups and items appear in the left sidebar.
Choose which level groups appear on the: Choose which level groups appear on the map and how each renders.
Choose which root tags are available whe: Choose which root tags are available when tagging bookmarks in this category. Leave all unchecked to hide the tag picker entirely for this category.
Classify groups by kind (e.g. Company, P: Classify groups by kind (e.g. Company, Podcast, Doujin Circle). Click one to view or edit it.
Classify how bookmarks relate to one ano: Classify how bookmarks relate to one another. Apply these types when linking bookmarks in the Relationships editor.
Classify what language a bookmark's cont: Classify what language a bookmark's content is in. Auto-detected from a scanned page, ISBN lookup, or YouTube video when possible.
Clean up orphaned records left behind wh: Clean up orphaned records left behind when their parent is deleted.
Color pin levels too (otherwise pins sta: Color pin levels too (otherwise pins stay gray)
Conditions that decide whether this rule: Conditions that decide whether this rule applies.
Configure a prompt for AI summarization: Configure a prompt for AI summarization and mark queued bookmarks as summarized.
Configure how bookmark URLs are cleaned: Configure how bookmark URLs are cleaned up and how shortened links are handled.
Configure the built-in \"Cropped\" ratio: Configure the built-in "Cropped" ratio and add custom named ratios to the aspect picker.

Configure the content and sections that: Configure the content and sections that appear on your homepage.

Control how bookmark cards display, base: Control how bookmark cards display, based on prioritized rules. Drag to set priority.
Control how romanized names/titles are s: Control how romanized names/titles are shown and sorted. When an item has a romanized form it is always shown de-emphasized after the primary label.
Control how the right-hand drawer opens.
Control the size of images and video emb: Control the size of images and video embeds on the bookmark detail page.
Copy your API key from Kavita under User: Copy your API key from Kavita under User Settings → API Key (older versions: 3rd Party Clients). The server exchanges it for a session token; it is never sent to the browser.

Create a book, movie, TV show, episode,: Create a book, movie, TV show, episode, album, or track — or look it up on Kavita or Plex to fill in its details automatically.
Create a location, including its parent: Create a location, including its parent chain if needed.
Create a media type under the current on: Create a media type under the current one — you can fill in the rest from its edit page.

Create a subtag under the current tag.
Create an entry under the current one —: Create an entry under the current one — you can fill in the rest from its edit page.

Custom properties checked here are avail: Custom properties checked here are available on bookmarks in this category.
Custom property values for this bookmark: Custom property values for this bookmark.
Defaults pre-filled into a bookmark’s “P: Defaults pre-filled into a bookmark’s “Page screenshot” capture controls on its Edit → Image tab. Changing these does not affect screenshots already taken.
Define custom properties to attach to bo: Define custom properties to attach to bookmarks. Each property becomes a filter on the bookmarks page — a combobox for tiered tags, a range slider for numbers.
Define rules that evaluate each inbox ca: Define rules that evaluate each inbox candidate's URL. The first matching rule automatically approves, rejects, or blocks the item. Unmatched items stay pending.
Define rules that match a bookmark's tit: Define rules that match a bookmark's title or website and prefill its category, tags, and custom properties when you add it. Select a rule to edit it, or create a new one.
Define the list of choices and how they: Define the list of choices and how they display.
Deleting {{entityName}} is permanent and: Deleting {{entityName}} is permanent and cannot be undone.
Detail properties (runtime, progress, se: Detail properties (runtime, progress, sections…) are hidden from the quick Add form by default and are better filled after creation. They stay editable on a bookmark's edit Properties tab regardless of the choice here.
Diagnostic detail for this table — copy: Diagnostic detail for this table — copy it to paste into an LLM when investigating why it's bulky.
Directional (reads parent → child rather: Directional (reads parent → child rather than symmetric)
Directional types read as parent → child: Directional types read as parent → child and power the Hierarchy view; symmetric types read the same from either bookmark.
Disk space used by each table in the Pos: Disk space used by each table in the PostgreSQL database (including its indexes). Row counts are estimates. Click a table to see diagnostic detail, or a column header to sort.
Display name shown next to the Active ba: Display name shown next to the Active badge above (e.g.
Display rules whose conditions match thi: Display rules whose conditions match this item, in priority order. Higher-priority rules (set on the Card Display Rules page) win. Open a rule to edit it — changes apply everywhere.
Domains of URL shorteners that can’t be: Domains of URL shorteners that can’t be tied to a specific site (e.g. bit.ly, t.co). When you add a link on one of these, the form nudges you to paste the full URL instead.
Domains whose redirect chains should nev: Domains whose redirect chains should never be followed when scanning a bookmark URL or processing newsletter imports (e.g. docs.google.com). Add a parent domain to cover all its subdomains (e.g. google.com covers docs.google.com and sheets.google.com).
Don't show this section when no bookmark: Don't show this section when no bookmarks match its filter.

Drag each field onto a zone. Image corners overlay it on the card image; anything left in “Available” is hidden.
Each enabled custom property can be show: Each enabled custom property can be shown by default or added on demand.
Each section shows bookmarks matching it: Each section shows bookmarks matching its filter, in the order you set here. Drag to reorder.

Edit buttons open the item’s full page b: Edit buttons open the item’s full page by default. Hold this key while clicking an Edit button to open the item in the right-hand drawer instead.

Edit the URL field above to replace it w: Edit the URL field above to replace it with the final destination link.

Edit the general details, options, categ: Edit the general details, options, categories, display, and autofill rules for this property.
Edit the group's name, website, group ty: Edit the group's name, website, group type, image, Plex link, year, and album credits.
Edit the name, description, and sidebar: Edit the name, description, and sidebar visibility for this filter.

Every channel already has an avatar.
Every image stored in the bucket. Run a: Every image stored in the bucket. Run a scan to catalog what's in storage. When a bookmark is deleted its image is preserved here as an orphan — use "Attach" to re-link it to another bookmark, or delete it to reclaim the space.
Every title condition needs a pattern.
Everything is already in sync with the s: Everything is already in sync with the source.
Everything that decides what this map dr: Everything that decides what this map draws — props, resolved settings, the Levels overlay state, and a per-location outcome (rendered vs. why it’s hidden). Copy it to share when something doesn’t show up.
Existing bookmarks this rule currently a: Existing bookmarks this rule currently applies to.
Extra names this site appends to titles: Extra names this site appends to titles (e.g. "Example", "EN"). Stripped automatically when a bookmark title is fetched.
Fetch a channel avatar (from its public: Fetch a channel avatar (from its public channel page) for every YouTube channel that doesn’t have one yet. Runs as a background job, batched to avoid YouTube rate-limiting — progress shows in the header while it runs.
Fetch a page preview image for every boo: Fetch a page preview image for every bookmark that doesn’t have one yet. The same job as on the
Fetch the image when a bookmark is saved: Fetch the image when a bookmark is saved
Fetch the title when the URL field loses: Fetch the title when the URL field loses focus
Filter map to this place type: Filter map to this place type
Find matching bookmarks missing this rul: Find matching bookmarks missing this rule's properties and apply them.
Flag this site when its redirects resolv: Flag this site when its redirects resolve unreliably. Flagged bookmarks appear in Settings → Redirect Failures for URL correction.
Flip which end of the palette is used fo: Flip which end of the palette is used for your largest level
For URLs whose path ends with the suffix: For URLs whose path ends with the suffix, keep only these query params (comma-separated) and strip the rest. Leave the path blank to match any path. With rules set, params aren’t kept unless whitelisted.
For matching paths, only these query par: For matching paths, only these query params are kept; the rest are stripped.
Franchises and IP groupings (e.g. a book: Franchises and IP groupings (e.g. a book series). Group related books under a media property. Click one to view or edit it.

Give this set of filters a name so you c: Give this set of filters a name so you can apply it again later.
Group bookmarks by category. Click a cat: Group bookmarks by category. Click a category to view it, or edit it for tiered tags, custom properties and autofill rules.
Grouped properties render together on bo: Grouped properties render together on bookmark pages and in the listings filters.
Hides some levels by default on maps anc: Hides some levels by default on maps anchored at this level
Hides the website pill on a card that al: Hides the website pill on a card that also has a YouTube channel.

Higher numbers win when rules conflict o: Higher numbers win when rules conflict on the category.
How a matching bookmark card looks with: How a matching bookmark card looks with these display settings.
How content offers a language — e.g. Dub: How content offers a language — e.g. Dub, Subtitles, Explanations.
How each card-body section arranges its: How each card-body section arranges its fields: inline flow (Flex) or a two-column grid.
How many bookmarks each listing page sho: How many bookmarks each listing page shows before paginating. Applies to the Bookmarks page and every entity-scoped bookmark listing (categories, tags, websites, media types, YouTube channels, …).
How matching bookmark cards are shown.
How matching bookmark cards are shown. U: How matching bookmark cards are shown. Unset attributes inherit from lower-priority rules.
How this category's bookmark listing is: How this category's bookmark listing is laid out.
How this section's bookmarks are laid ou: How this section's bookmarks are laid out on the homepage.
ISBN lookups try Open Library first, the: ISBN lookups try Open Library first, then fall back to the keyless Google Books API for broader coverage.
ISBN {{isbn}} will be saved with this bo: ISBN {{isbn}} will be saved with this book.
ISO 639-1 code, used to match autofetche: ISO 639-1 code, used to match autofetched languages from scans and ISBN lookups.
ISO code, usage counts, and metadata.
Import name and the default category, ta: Import name and the default category, tags, and media type applied to imported bookmarks.
Import queued — links will appear in you: Import queued — links will appear in your Inbox as they're processed.
Import the linked Kavita series' cover a: Import the linked Kavita series' cover as the main image
Import the linked Plex item's poster as: Import the linked Plex item's poster as the main image
Import the linked book's table of conten: Import the linked book's table of contents (replaces the current list)
Inbox items from newsletter imports whos: Inbox items from newsletter imports whose newsletter was deleted. Extension quick-saves are excluded. Created bookmarks are kept.
Individual albums, optionally grouped un: Individual albums, optionally grouped under a media property and linked to a Plex item. Bookmarks link to an album here. Click one to view or edit it.
Individual books, optionally grouped und: Individual books, optionally grouped under a media property and linked to a Kavita series. Bookmarks link to a book here. Click one to view or edit it.
Individual episodes, optionally grouped: Individual episodes, optionally grouped under a media property and linked to a Plex item. Bookmarks link to an episode here. Click one to view or edit it.
Individual movies, optionally grouped un: Individual movies, optionally grouped under a media property and linked to a Plex item. Bookmarks link to a movie here. Click one to view or edit it.
Individual podcasts, optionally grouped: Individual podcasts, optionally grouped under a media property and synced from their RSS feed. Bookmarks link to a podcast here. Click one to view or edit it.
Individual tracks, optionally grouped un: Individual tracks, optionally grouped under a media property and linked to a Plex item. Bookmarks link to a track here. Click one to view or edit it.
Inheriting card fields from lower-priori: Inheriting card fields from lower-priority rules.
Inheriting section layout from lower-pri: Inheriting section layout from lower-priority rules.
Instagram reel archiving isn't configure: Instagram reel archiving isn't configured. Set up object storage (and, optionally, a Browserless endpoint) to enable it — see Settings → Connectors.
Install a bookmarklet to save the curren: Install a bookmarklet to save the current page.
Kavita has {{label}}: {{value}}. eeSimpl: Kavita has {{label}}: {{value}}. eeSimple Bookmarks can't write to Kavita — update it there manually if this should change.
Keep any of the images found on the page: Keep any of the images found on the page (or drag, paste, or add your own), then click the
Keyless fallback for the Locations geoco: Keyless fallback for the Locations geocoder. When Nominatim has no entry for a place — typically a traditional or natural region with no administrative boundary, e.g. 中国地方 (Chūgoku region) — Wikidata supplies its coordinates, country, and ancestor chain, and fills the area outline (its linked OpenStreetMap relation, or composed from the region's constituent units).
Keyless oEmbed lookups return clean, str: Keyless oEmbed lookups return clean, structured metadata for many media URLs — no scraping, no API key. Sites not listed here are still covered when they advertise an oEmbed endpoint (autodiscovery via the page's
Keyless place lookup for the Locations t: Keyless place lookup for the Locations taxonomy, powered by OpenStreetMap Nominatim. Looking up a place resolves its coordinates, country, and a map link.
Keyless podcast metadata: search Apple P: Keyless podcast metadata: search Apple Podcasts (the iTunes Search API) or Pocket Casts, or paste an RSS/XML feed URL, to autofill a podcast's title, author, artwork, and description — and re-pull them any time with "Sync from source". Its page link on each service is found by matching the feed across directories; Spotify has no keyless search, so paste its link. No API key, nothing leaves the box beyond the feed/directory lookups.

Optional. Set the TOKEN env var on your: Optional. Set the TOKEN env var on your Browserless container and enter the same value here. Sent as an Authorization: Bearer header.
Optional. When a base URL and API key ar: Optional. When a base URL and API key are configured, bookmarks can be linked to a series on your self-hosted Kavita ebook/manga server — with a "View on Kavita" link-out and series cover import. Off by default; series searches and cover fetches are proxied by the server, so the API key never reaches the browser.
Optional. When a base URL and token are: Optional. When a base URL and token are configured, bookmarks can be linked to an item on your self-hosted Plex media server (movie, TV show, music, …) — with a "View on Plex" link-out and poster import. Off by default; item searches and poster fetches are proxied by the server, so the token never reaches the browser.
Optional. When a base URL is configured,: Optional. When a base URL is configured, bookmarks link out to your self-hosted ArchiveBox web archive — viewing the archived snapshot of a page and archiving it on demand. Off by default; link-out only, so no token is sent and nothing leaves the box automatically.
Optional. When configured with a Browser: Optional. When configured with a Browserless endpoint, JS-rendered and bot-protected pages are resolved by the hosted browser. Off by default — URLs are only sent to Browserless when an endpoint is configured, and the app falls back to the direct scrape otherwise.
Optional. When object storage is configu: Optional. When object storage is configured, a saved Instagram reel can be archived on demand — the reel's video is captured into your own object storage so it survives the reel being deleted from Instagram. Self-contained; the video is fetched and stored on the box, not linked out. The video URL is found keylessly via Instagram's public embed page; a configured Browserless endpoint is used as a fallback for reels it doesn't expose.
Optionally add higher-level locations, f: Optionally add higher-level locations, from the immediate parent up to the root. Pick an existing location to reuse it, or fill in a new one — new ancestors are created (or reused by name) when you save.

Title, thumbnail, and channel always com: Title, thumbnail, and channel always come from YouTube's keyless oEmbed endpoint. Adding a YouTube Data API v3 key makes duration, publish date, description, and channel avatars come from the stable API instead of scraping YouTube's pages, which is more reliable — YouTube increasingly blocks non-browser requests to its pages. Saves on blur.

When disabled, this property will not ap: When disabled, this property will not appear in the category defaults editor.
When enabled, leaving the URL field whil: When enabled, leaving the URL field while adding a bookmark fetches the page’s title automatically. You can always fetch it manually with the button next to the Name field.
When enabled, saving a bookmark whose ti: When enabled, saving a bookmark whose title contains an existing location’s name automatically applies that location. Matching is case-insensitive and only counts whole words.

When enabled, saving a bookmark whose title contains an existing tag’s name automatically applies that tag. Matching is case-insensitive and only counts whole words, so a tag named “art” won’t match “Martin”.
When enabled, the Images section of the: When enabled, the Images section of the Add Bookmark form is collapsed by default — the page’s preview image will be fetched automatically when you save. Disable to always show the Images section expanded.
When on, the drawer docks as a persisten: When on, the drawer docks as a persistent column on the right side. The pin button inside the drawer lets you toggle this on the fly.
When scanning a URL for images, any cand: When scanning a URL for images, any candidate whose URL matches one of these patterns is dropped before it reaches the Add Bookmark picker. A pattern is a case-insensitive substring (e.g.
When this is a map’s current level, only: When this is a map’s current level, only the checked levels show by default. The “Show” buttons above still bound the range — unchecking a level removes it from the default — and a map’s Levels overlay can still turn any level back on for that map.
Where this property appears and whether: Where this property appears and whether it's editable from the card menu or CMD+K.
Which bookmarks this rule applies to.
Which bookmarks this rule applies to. Co: Which bookmarks this rule applies to. Combine conditions with AND/OR.
Which existing bookmarks match this rule: Which existing bookmarks match this rule.
Which image source the bookmark's cover: Which image source the bookmark's cover displays
Which levels a place’s map (or a bookmar: Which levels a place’s map (or a bookmark’s map, when one of its tagged locations is this level) shows by default when this is its own level — broader levels too, only this level, or narrower levels. The map’s Levels overlay edits the same default.
Which service this podcast links out to: Which service this podcast links out to (detail page + bookmark cards).

instance. When set, bookmarks gain links: instance. When set, bookmarks gain links to view the archived snapshot of their page and to archive it on demand. Link-out only — no token is sent, and the links open against your own ArchiveBox in a new tab. Saves on blur.
media server. When set, bookmarks can be: media server. When set, bookmarks can be linked to a movie, show, or track, gain a "View on Plex" link-out, and can import the item's poster as their image. The token stays on the server — searches and poster fetches are proxied so it never reaches the browser. Each field saves on blur.
on this host in a new tab.
server. When set, bookmarks can be linke: server. When set, bookmarks can be linked to a Kavita series, gain a "View on Kavita" link-out, and can import the series cover as their image. The API key stays on the server — searches and cover fetches are proxied so it never reaches the browser. Each field saves on blur.
